result:
All confirmed as genuine function-pointer members. Results:

**Definition location:** `struct wpa_driver_ops` — `src/drivers/driver.h:1633` (struct body spans lines 1633–3442, closing at `};` on line 3442).

**Total function-pointer fields: 142**

Verified via:
- `grep -n "^struct wpa_driver_ops" src/drivers/driver.h` → line 1633
- LSP `goToDefinition` at that location confirmed it resolves to itself (i.e., this is the canonical definition, not a forward declaration).
- Counted all struct members matching the function-pointer-declaration pattern `(*name)(` within the struct body (lines 1633–3442) → 142 matches.
- Checked for any declarations split across lines (where `(*name)` appears alone at end of line with args wrapped to the next line) — none found, so no double-counting or missed entries.

Sample fields (first few / last few, out of 142): `get_bssid`, `get_ssid`, `set_key`, `init`, `deinit`, … `leave_mesh`, `do_acs`, `set_band`, `get_pref_freq_list`, `set_prob_oper_freq`.
